summary: I was born on a farm down here in Clarke County, Georgia, though I don't know exactly when because I didn't know much about my mother. I spent most of my younger days working in the fields before moving to town about sixteen years ago. Clarke County is where my roots are, and it shaped much of my early life before I started nursing and taking in washing in town.
cues: moved to town sixteen years ago

summary: I went to school at Morton's Chapel, which was a church house where we had classes during the week and church on Sundays. It was a simple place, a big quarter and a little chapel, and it served as both our school and Sunday school. Mr. Morton, who gave the chapel, was a fine man, and I remember him well from those days before I got married and started my family.

My daughter lives in Atlanta, and she’s planning to move her furniture when she gets a room there. I’ve been in town for about sixteen years now, and Atlanta represents a place where my family is trying to establish themselves. Though I live here with my washing and nursing work, Atlanta is where my daughter is making her way.
